commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The component lacks any hover or interactive feedback states for the forecast items.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover background or scale effect to the forecast stack items to indicate they are data points.

issues.description: Lack of ARIA labels for weather icons; screen readers will interpret emojis inconsistently.
issues.fix: Add 'aria-label' attributes to the icon text elements describing the weather condition.

issues.description: The forecast strip lacks a container or clear grouping, making it feel slightly detached from the main card.
issues.fix: Wrap forecast items in a slightly distinct background color or subtle hover-enabled border to improve visual grouping.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses the design system appropriately, and effectively maps all required props. Improvements in accessibility (ARIA roles for icons) and minor visual refinements to the forecast section would elevate this to a production-ready state.

evaluation.critique: The component has good data handling and hierarchy, but suffers from inconsistent design token usage—mixing var() with fallback hex colors and using hardcoded padding (24) instead of spacing tokens. Polish is adequate but lacks hover states and transitions that would enhance interactivity.
